title: centaurea × castellano-manchensis
intro: Centaurea × castellano-manchensis, also known as Montesma's knapweed, is a hybrid plant in the family Asteraceae, derived from the cross between Centaurea monticola and Centaurea calcitrapa. The medicinal properties of Centaurea × castellano-manchensis are attributed to its alkaloid and flavonoid content, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. This plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, menstrual cramps, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Centaurea × castellano-manchensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
